explanation: The property states that globally (G), if the controller is in PSV mode, the expiratory phase is active, a spontaneous breath is not detected (drop(PAW) > ITS_PSV is false), it is within the [min_exp_time_psv : apnea lag] interval, and the Expiratory Pause is set by the GUI, then an Expiratory Pause shall start in the next state (X). The template G(bool_exp1 -> X bool_exp3) precisely captures this cause-effect relationship for the immediate next step.

The property states that globally, if the system is in PSV Mode, the controller is in the expiratory phase, a spontaneous breath is not detected (i.e., drop(PAW) > ITS_PSV is false), it is within the specified expiratory interval, and the 'Expiratory Pause' button is pressed, then an 'Expiratory Pause' must start in the very next state. This is captured by a global implication (G) where the complex antecedent is combined into 'bool_exp2' and the consequent 'expiratoryPause' is expected in the next state (X bool_exp3).

The property describes a conditional response: if a specific set of conditions (the controller is in the expiratory phase, a spontaneous breath is not detected, the current time is within the [min_exp_time_psv : apnea lag] interval, and the Expiratory Pause is set by the GUI) are met, then an Expiratory Pause must start in the very next state. This 'globally, if A then next B' pattern is precisely captured by LTL template ID 11. 'bool_exp2' encapsulates all the combined antecedent conditions, and 'bool_exp3' represents the initiation of the Expiratory Pause in the subsequent state.

The property states that globally, if the system is in PSV mode, the controller is in the expiration phase, a spontaneous breath is not detected (i.e., drop(PAW) > ITS_PSV is false), the current time within the expiration phase is within the interval [min_exp_time_psv : apnea lag], and the Expiratory Pause is set by the GUI, then an Expiratory Pause shall start in the very next state. This corresponds to a standard implication where multiple conditions must hold in the current state for a specific event to occur in the next state.

The property states that globally, whenever the controller is in the expiration phase, a spontaneous breath is not detected (drop(PAW) > ITS_PSV is false), it is within the specified time interval, AND the Expiratory Pause is set by the GUI, then an Expiratory Pause shall start, meaning it will be active in the next state. This is captured by the temporal implication `G (trigger_condition -> X expiratoryPause)`.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the controller is in PSV mode, is in the expiratory phase, a spontaneous breath is not detected (i.e., drop(PAW) > ITS_PSV is false), and is within the interval [min_exp_time_psv : apnea lag], AND the Expiratory Pause is set by the GUI, then in the next state (X), an Expiratory Pause shall start. This captures the conditional obligation for the Expiratory Pause to commence immediately after the specified conditions are met.

The property states that globally, if the system is in PSV mode, the controller is in the expiration phase, a spontaneous breath is not detected (i.e., drop(PAW) > ITS_PSV is false), the current time in the expiration phase is within the specified interval, and the Expiratory Pause is set by the GUI, then an Expiratory Pause shall start in the next state. This is captured by a global implication (G) where the combined conditions form the antecedent and the start of the expiratory pause in the next state (X) forms the consequent.

The property states that globally, if the controller is in the expiration phase, a spontaneous breath is not detected (i.e., drop(PAW) > ITS_PSV is false), it is within the time interval [min_exp_time_psv : apnea lag], and the Expiratory Pause is set by the GUI, then an Expiratory Pause shall be active. This is a standard implication where the conjunction of all conditions forms the antecedent (bool_exp1) and the desired state (Expiratory Pause) forms the consequent (bool_exp3).

The property states that globally (G), if all the specified conditions are met (the controller is in PSV mode, in expiration phase, a spontaneous breath is not detected, it's within the specific time interval, and the Expiratory Pause is set by the GUI), then an Expiratory Pause shall start in the very next state (X). This directly maps to LTL template 31 which is of the form G (bool_exp1 -> (X bool_exp3)).

The property states that globally (G), if the controller is in the expiratory phase, a spontaneous breath is not detected (drop(PAW) > ITS_PSV is false), it is within the specified expiration time interval [min_exp_time_psv : apnea lag], and the Expiratory Pause is set by the GUI (bool_exp1), then an Expiratory Pause (bool_exp3) shall start in the next state (X). This directly translates the 'if ... then ... shall start' requirement into a global implication with a next operator.
